channel.title: FM and Diff - MIT CSAIL on Utsab's Digital Garden
channel.description: Recent content in FM and Diff - MIT CSAIL on Utsab's Digital Garden

item.title: Lecture-1
item.description: Flow and Diffusion Models We start by defining a trajectory. Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs): The mathematical foundation for flow models. Trajectory: The solution to an ODE. It is a function that maps time $t$ to a specific location in a $d$-dimensional space. - Function: $X : [0, 1] \to \mathbb{R}^d, \quad t \mapsto X_t$ Vector Field ($u$): The rule that defines an ODE. It assigns a specific velocity vector to every point in space at any given time - Function: $u : \mathbb{R}^d \times [0, 1] \to \mathbb{R}^d, \quad (x, t) \mapsto u_t(x)$

item.title: Lecture-2
item.description: How do they guarantee that the learnt marginal vector field will transform the distribution such that when sampled later it creates sensible data samples?
